Cultural.rationale: Consistent emphasis on controlled immigration, cultural preservation, and British values in manifestos and legislation since 2010.

sources.title: Conservative Party Manifesto 2024
sources.relevantText: We will introduce an annual cap on work and family visas, ensuring that immigration is sustainable and serves the national interest.
sources.citation: Conservative Party, 'Clear Plan. Bold Action. Secure Future.', 2024, p. 23

sources.title: Illegal Migration Act 2023
sources.relevantText: An Act to make provision for and in connection with the removal from the United Kingdom of persons who have entered or arrived in breach of immigration control.

Military.rationale: Consistently pro-NATO, increased defence spending commitments, support for Ukraine, and interventionist foreign policy.

sources.title: Conservative Party Manifesto 2024
sources.relevantText: We will increase defence spending to 2.5% of GDP by 2030 and maintain our unwavering support for NATO.
sources.citation: Conservative Party, 'Clear Plan. Bold Action. Secure Future.', 2024, p. 45

sources.title: Defence Spending Motion
sources.relevantText: Motion to increase defence spending to 2.5% of GDP: Ayes 312, Noes 201

Sovereignty.rationale: Brexit delivered under Conservative government, but ongoing engagement with international frameworks (CPTPP, NATO, UN).

sources.title: European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018
sources.relevantText: An Act to repeal the European Communities Act 1972 and make other provision in connection with the withdrawal of the United Kingdom from the EU.

sources.title: Accession to Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP)
sources.relevantText: The UK becomes the first new member of CPTPP since its founding, demonstrating commitment to multilateral trade.
sources.citation: Department for Business and Trade press release, 16 July 2023

Economic.rationale: Post-Brexit trade liberalisation and free-market rhetoric, tempered by industrial strategy and state intervention during crises.

sources.title: UK-Australia Free Trade Agreement
sources.relevantText: The agreement removes tariffs on 99.9% of goods, demonstrating the party's commitment to liberal trade.

sources.title: Subsidy Control Act 2022
sources.relevantText: An Act to make provision for the control of subsidies by public authorities.

Class.rationale: Pro-business, anti-union legislation (Minimum Service Levels Act), reduction of worker protections, tax policies favouring capital over labour.

sources.title: Strikes (Minimum Service Levels) Act 2023
sources.relevantText: An Act to make provision about minimum levels of service that must be provided during strikes.

sources.title: Second Reading — Strikes (Minimum Service Levels) Bill
